purpose: Validate JSON payloads against supplied JSON Schema documents and return deterministic, structured validation errors for API and configuration boundaries.

endpoints.description: Validate a JSON value against a JSON Schema document supplied in the request.

dependencies.purpose: Compile and evaluate JSON Schema draft 2020-12 validation rules
Validate standard JSON Schema string formats such as email and uri

sidecar_specification.purpose: JSON Schema validation service for untrusted payloads and configuration objects.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when a caller has a JSON Schema and needs a payload checked before further processing.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for semantic business validation, database uniqueness checks, or schemas that require external remote references.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Build a local JSON Schema object. 2) Submit schema and data to /validate. 3) If valid is false, return errors to the caller or remediation agent.

failure_modes.recovery: Fix the schema document before retrying validation

failure_modes.symptom: 200 response with valid false and errors array
failure_modes.recovery: Correct the payload fields listed in errors

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls
Must not fetch remote JSON Schema references

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use Ajv from ajv/dist/2020.js with allErrors controlled by the request. Add ajv-formats locally. Compile the supplied schema per request or cache by stable JSON string hash. Return Ajv errors mapped to { path: instancePath || '/', message, keyword }.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Remote $ref values can trigger unsafe assumptions if loadSchema is enabled. Keep validation synchronous and do not configure remote schema loading.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Use Ajv validate(data) and map validate.errors without throwing for normal validation failures.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not use eval, do not mutate the input payload, and do not resolve schemas over HTTP.
